Out of office protocol. So this is if you or one of your teammates is going on vacation or sick or really whatever you're out of the office for some reason. Okay so one thing you remember is that the best way to kill morale in a workplace is to go on vacation without having prepared anyone for your departure. This means you as a leader leaving without preparing people as well as it's going to frustrate you extremely if people on your team don't do this for you as well and you as a leader have to be the example so these are the things that you need to do when you are going out of town and if you're not going to prepare for being out of office then just don't go out of office because it's going to you fuck shit up. Okay so that being said we have a document that you can download for this but I just wanted to go over the main points so that you don't miss them. Okay number one is you need to email your team the week prior to you leaving. Why? Because they don't remember that you have a vacation. You could have told them a month ago two months ago even a week ago. Email them again letting them know the week prior to you leaving. Okay that way they can think up anything that they need done or any questions they have for you prior to that. Two is appointing a point of contact while you're out for decision making so whether it's regarding member exits, billing, you know, if somebody on the team messes up and they need a write-up whatever it may be that you're responsible for you need to appoint that decision-making power to someone else. This is a great opportunity for you to set use this as almost like a stretch goal for someone on your team if you feel like they have potential to take over some of the stuff you're doing right now. Right so I would look at it as a great opportunity. Three is explain and define what an emergency is that would deem them reaching out to you. Is it someone quits? Is it a member gets hurt? Is it something softer like a member wants to leave? Like what is it that you deem necessary that they should reach out to you? Right four is create a calendar invite for your whole team alerting them of your absence. So what this means is you send a block that says you know Layla out of town Layla out of office and it appears on all of your teammates calendars so that every day they remember oh yeah she's out of office she's out of office right. Five is declining meetings that you would normally attend and he zooms and he huddles etc and then six kind of going hand in hand which is appoint someone else to lead the meetings that you normally run. Again this could be a stretch goal for someone to who you feel could be good for someone taking over some of what you're doing right now. It's a great opportunity for that person to show you that they are capable of running meetings they're capable of keeping things going and that the team doesn't come to a halt when you're gone but things keep moving forward right and that is why this is so important because if you put all these things into place things will continue to move forward right and you will not come back to like a mess or chaos right. Eight ask your team not to flood your inbox so something that I made a mistake of you know in the beginning was I was like yeah just continue you know sending me whatever you normally would and that is really overwhelming if you come back and you have you know I don't know I think I had like a hundred and seventeen unread emails okay. Nine make sure that your one-on-ones are actually email updates so if you feel like I know for me when I'm gone I still want to know what's going on right. I'm still gonna check my email probably in the morning for like 20 minutes and so if that's how you feel as well then I suggest that you ask anyone that you would have normally been having a one-on-one with to just email you an update okay and I've included an example of what you could ask them to send you. Ten block off schedule once in a community so the worst thing you could do is to have left one of your calendars for bookings up and then gone out town I heard that from quite a few gym lords at the last summit we had and then lastly if you have a work Facebook profile or especially for your teammates who you own their Facebook meaning they use their work email to create their Facebook accounts like we suggest in the beginning of this training then you have them change their profile picture to an out-of-office sign so that anyone who's client-facing the clients now know that you're out of office okay so do all of these things and you can rest easy when you're out of town and you will not come back to chaos.
